html:
[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 言未毕 - Means 'while speaking was not yet finished.'
  • 捧一册至 - Means 'holding a scroll came.'
  • 上书 - Means 'writing on top' or 'inscribed on top.'
  • 应将前定天机漏泄 - Means 'should disclose the previously determined heavenly secrets.'
  • 俾世人共晓 - Means 'so that all people may understand.'
  • 仍送归浙江 - Means 'still send back to Zhejiang.'
  • 文木拜谢 - Means 'Wenmu bowed and thanked.'
  • 临别泣下 - Means 'at parting wept.'
  • 胡然 - Means 'why so?'
  • 十二万年之后 - Means 'after twelve million years.'
  • 我与汝又会于此矣 - Means 'I and you will meet again here.'
  • 何必泣为 - Means 'why cry for?'
  • 我错，我错 - Means 'I was wrong, I was wrong.'
  • 此一泣 - Means 'this one cry.'
  • 亦是十二万年中原有两条眼泪 - Means 'is also the two tears that originally existed in twelve million years.'
  • 故照样誊录 - Means 'so it is copied verbatim.'
  • 我不必劝止也 - Means 'I do not need to stop you.'
  • 王年寿 - Means 'the king's lifespan.'
  • 王与第一次盘古同生 - Means 'the king and the first Pangu were born together.'
  • 不与第千万次盘古同死 - Means 'not with the thousandth Pangu dying together.'
  • 乾坤毁时 - Means 'when heaven and earth are destroyed.'
  • 王将安归 - Means 'where will the king go?'
  • 沙身也 - Means 'body of sand.'
  • 历劫不坏 - Means 'endures through calamities without being destroyed.'
  • 万物毁坏，变为泥沙而极矣 - Means 'all things are destroyed and become mud and sand, reaching their limit.'
  • 我先居于极坏之处 - Means 'I first reside in the most desolate place.'
  • 劫火不能烧 - Means 'calamitous fire cannot burn.'
  • 洪水不能淹 - Means 'floods cannot drown.'
  • 惟为恶风所吹荡 - Means 'only blown away by evil winds.'
  • 上至九天，下至九渊 - Means 'up to the ninth heaven, down to the ninth abyss.'
  • 殊觉劳顿 - Means 'feels very tiring.'
  • 每每枯坐数万年 - Means 'often sit dry for tens of thousands of years.'
  • 等盘古出世 - Means 'waiting for Pangu to be born.'
  • 觉日子太多，殊可厌耳 - Means 'feel the days are too many, very annoying indeed.'
  • 口嘘气吹文木 - Means 'exhaling breath to blow Wenmu.'
  • 仍至海船上 - Means 'still return to the ship at sea.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 文木 - Refers to Wenmu, a character in the text.
  • 王 - Refers to the king in the text.
  • 毗骞国 - Refers to Biquan Kingdom, a mythical place.
  • 盘古 - Refers to Pangu, a mythological figure in Chinese mythology who separated the sky and the earth.
  • 乾坤 - Refers to heaven and earth.
  • 九天 - Refers to the ninth heaven, a concept in Chinese cosmology.
  • 九渊 - Refers to the ninth abyss, a concept in Chinese cosmology.

Proper Nouns

  • 文木 - A character in the text.
  • 王 - The king in the text.
  • 毗骞国 - Biquan Kingdom, a mythical place.
  • 盘古 - Pangu, a mythological figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 乾坤 - Heaven and earth.
  • 九天 - The ninth heaven.
  • 九渊 - The ninth abyss.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 人但知万事前定 - Common philosophical statement about fate; people only know that all things are predestined.
  • 而不知所以前定之故 - Introduces a deeper inquiry into the cause of predestination; but they do not know the reason why it is predestined.
  • 今得是说，方始豁然 - Expresses a moment of enlightenment; now that I have heard this explanation, I finally understand clearly.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 毛西河先生 - Historical figure known for his scholarship and contributions to literature; Mr. Mao Xihé.
  • 明季 - Historical reference to the late Ming dynasty; during the late Ming dynasty.
  • 华山寺 - Specific temple in China; Huashan Temple.
  • 道人 - Term often used to describe a person who follows Taoist practices; applied to the pig in this context.
  • 住持 - Head of a Buddhist temple; abbot.
  • 说法 - Common activity for Buddhist monks; preaching or teaching.

Proper Nouns

  • 毛西河先生 - Historical figure; Mr. Mao Xihé.
  • 华山寺 - Specific temple; Huashan Temple.
  • 湛一和尚 - Abbot of Huashan Temple; Venerable Zhan Yi.
  • 渭 - Disciple of Venerable Zhan Yi; Wei.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 已而 - Temporal marker meaning 'after a while' or 'later'.
  • 乃私埋之 - '乃' indicates a consequential action, '私' suggests secrecy or personal action, and '埋之' means 'to bury it.' Together, this phrase means 'then secretly buried it.'
  • 湛一归 - '湛一' is a proper name, and '归' means 'returned home.'
  • 问猪死作何处分 - '问' means 'asked,' '猪死' means 'the pig died,' and '作何处分' means 'what was done with it.' This phrase means 'asked what had been done with the dead pig.'
  • 众僧以实告 - '众僧' means 'the monks,' '以实告' means 'told the truth.' This phrase means 'the monks told the truth.'
  • 佛法戒杀 - '佛法' means 'Buddhist teachings,' '戒杀' means 'forbid killing.' This phrase means 'Buddhist teachings forbid killing.'
  • 某等已埋葬之 - '某等' means 'we,' '已埋葬之' means 'have already buried it.' This phrase means 'we have already buried it.'
  • 吾负汝！吾负汝！ - '吾' means 'I,' '负' means 'betrayed' or 'failed,' and '汝' means 'you.' This phrase means 'I have failed you! I have failed you!'
  • 然此亦大数 - '然' means 'but,' '此' means 'this,' '亦' means 'also,' and '大数' means 'great destiny' or 'fate.' This phrase means 'but this is also part of great destiny.'
  • 无可挽回也 - '无' means 'not,' '可' means 'can,' '挽回' means 'undo' or 'reverse,' and '也' is a particle. This phrase means 'cannot be undone.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 崇祯间 - '崇祯' refers to the reign of Emperor Chongzhen of the Ming Dynasty, indicating the historical context.
  • 某村翰林郑曼阝素行端方 - '翰林' refers to a high-ranking scholar-official, and '郑曼阝' is a proper name. '素行端方' means 'always acted with integrity.'
  • 东林党籍中 - '东林党' refers to the Donglin Party, a political faction during the late Ming Dynasty.
  • 其舅吴某诬其杖母事 - '其舅' means 'his uncle,' '吴某' is a proper name, '诬' means 'accused falsely,' and '杖母事' means 'the incident of beating his mother.' This phrase means 'his uncle, Wu, falsely accused him of beating his mother.'
  • 凌迟处死 - '凌迟' is a form of execution known as 'death by slicing,' and '处死' means 'executed.' This phrase means 'executed by death by slicing.'
  • 天下冤之 - '天下' means 'the world' or 'the people,' '冤' means 'pity' or 'feel wronged,' and '之' is a particle. This phrase means 'the world felt pity for him.'
  • 湛一业已圆寂 - '湛一' is a proper name, '业已' means 'has already,' and '圆寂' means 'passed away' or 'died.' This phrase means 'Zhan Yi has already passed away.'
  • 众方服其通因果也 - '众' means 'the people,' '方' means 'now,' '服' means 'believe' or 'admire,' '其' means 'him,' '通因果' means 'understands cause and effect,' and '也' is a particle. This phrase means 'now people admire his understanding of cause and effect.'

Proper Nouns

  • 湛一 - A proper name.
  • 某村翰林郑曼阝 - A proper name, '郑曼阝' is a specific individual.
  • 东林党 - A political faction during the late Ming Dynasty.
  • 其舅吴某 - A proper name, '吴某' is a specific individual.
  • 崇祯 - The reign title of Emperor Chongzhen of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 圆寂 - A term used to describe the passing of a Buddhist monk.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先生謂赞臣曰 - A formal expression meaning 'the master told Zan Chen, saying.'
  • 君等家資雖富 - Means 'though you all are wealthy in family assets.'
  • 能煉丹，可加十倍 - Means 'if you can refine elixirs, you can multiply your wealth tenfold.'
  • 群兄弟惑其言 - Means 'the brothers were bewildered by his words.'
  • 置爐設灶 - Means 'set up furnaces and stoves.'
  • 各出銀母數千以求子金 - Means 'each contributed several thousand silver ingots to seek offspring gold.'
  • 素黠 - Means 'always cunning.'
  • 暗置銅於銀母中 - Means 'secretly placed copper in the silver ingots.'
  • 不與先生見 - Means 'without showing it to the master.'
  • 亡何炭熾 - Means 'soon the charcoal blazed.'
  • 風雷起於屋上 - Means 'a wind and thunder arose on the roof.'
  • 劈碎瓦數片 - Means 'shattered several tiles.'
  • 詢之，果然 - Means 'when asked, it turned out to be true.'
  • 合家駭服 - Means 'the whole family was terrified and convinced.'
  • 盤中鏗然，一錠墜下 - Means 'a clanging sound was heard in the tray, and a piece of gold fell.'
  • 連呼之，鏗鏗之聲不已 - Means 'continuing to call, the clanging sounds did not stop.'
  • 大錠小錠齊落於盤 - Means 'large and small pieces fell into the tray together.'
  • 煉大丹在深山中人迹不到之所，可致千萬 - Means 'refining the great elixir in a deep mountain where no one goes can yield millions.'
  • 盍隨我往江西廬山乎 - Means 'why not follow me to Mount Lu in Jiangxi?'
  • 石氏兄弟愈喜 - Means 'the brothers of the Shi family became even happier.'
  • 即載銀數萬隨先生往 - Means 'immediately loaded tens of thousands of silver and followed the master.'
  • 未半途，先生上岸去矣 - Means 'before halfway, the master went ashore and left.'
  • 夜，率大盜數十明火執杖來劫取銀 - Means 'at night, leading dozens of bandits with torches and weapons, they came to rob the silver.'
  • 毋怖，我雖盜魁，然颇有良心 - Means 'do not be afraid, though I am the leader of the bandits, I still have a conscience.'
  • 念汝等供養我甚誠，當留下千金，俾汝等還鄉 - Means 'thinking that you have been very sincere in supporting me, I will leave you a thousand gold pieces so you can return to your hometown.'
  • 於是，石家兄弟以全數與之，惘惘然歸 - Means 'thus, the brothers of the Shi family gave all the silver and returned home in a daze.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 煉丹 - Refers to alchemy, a practice in ancient China aimed at producing elixirs of immortality.
  • 銀母 - Refers to silver ingots, often used as a form of currency or wealth.
  • 子金 - Refers to offspring gold, a term for newly produced gold.
  • 假銀 - Refers to counterfeit silver, often used to deceive in alchemical practices.
  • 鬼神 - Refers to spirits or deities, often invoked in traditional Chinese beliefs.
  • 江西廬山 - Refers to Mount Lu in Jiangxi Province, a famous mountain known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
  • 盜魁 - Refers to the leader of a bandit group, often a figure of authority among criminals.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening phrase '先生謂赞臣曰' introduces the master speaking to Zan Chen, setting the context for the dialogue.
  • The clause '君等家資雖富，能煉丹，可加十倍' presents a conditional statement, emphasizing the potential wealth from alchemy.
  • The sequence '群兄弟惑其言，置爐設灶，各出銀母數千以求子金' describes the brothers' actions in response to the master's words, setting up the alchemical process.
  • The clause '素黠，暗置銅於銀母中，不與先生見' describes the cunning nature of one of the brothers and his deceitful act.
  • The sequence '亡何炭熾，風雷起於屋上，劈碎瓦數片' describes the dramatic and supernatural event that occurs during the alchemical process.
  • The clause '此必有假銀摻雜，致干鬼神怒' provides the master's explanation for the supernatural event, linking it to the presence of counterfeit silver.
  • The phrase '先生置銅盤於空中，呼曰：‘丹來。’' describes the master's demonstration of his supposed magical abilities.
  • The sequence '盤中鏗然，一錠墜下；連呼之，鏗鏗之聲不已，大錠小錠齊落於盤' describes the dramatic and seemingly magical outcome of the master's demonstration.
  • The clause '煉大丹在深山中人迹不到之所，可致千萬，盍隨我往江西廬山乎' presents the master's invitation to the brothers to join him in a more secluded and potentially more profitable alchemical venture.
  • The sequence '石氏兄弟愈喜，即載銀數萬隨先生往' describes the brothers' enthusiastic response to the master's invitation.
  • The phrase '未半途，先生上岸去矣' describes the master's abrupt departure, leaving the brothers in a vulnerable position.
  • The sequence '夜，率大盜數十明火執杖來劫取銀' describes the bandits' attack, revealing the master's true intentions.
  • The phrase '毋怖，我雖盜魁，然颇有良心' provides the bandit leader's justification for his actions, adding a layer of moral complexity.
  • The clause '念汝等供養我甚誠，當留下千金，俾汝等還鄉' explains the bandit leader's decision to leave some silver, showing a sense of gratitude.
  • The sequence '於是，石家兄弟以全數與之，惘惘然歸' describes the brothers' final actions and their state of mind upon returning home.

Proper Nouns

  • 先生 - Refers to the master, a figure of authority and expertise in alchemy.
  • 赞臣 - Refers to Zan Chen, a person to whom the master speaks.
  • 石氏兄弟 - Refers to the brothers of the Shi family, the main subjects of the story.
  • 江西廬山 - Refers to Mount Lu in Jiangxi Province, a specific location mentioned in the text.
  • 盜魁 - Refers to the leader of the bandit group, a key figure in the final part of the story.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 好食铜铁而不伤人 - Loves eating copper and iron but does not harm people; describes the creature's unique behavior.
  • 泪下如雨 - Cries like rain; describes the creature's emotional state.
  • 悲鸣而去 - Weeps and departs; describes the creature's final moments.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 貘 - Mythical creature in Chinese folklore, often depicted as a large, bear-like animal that eats metal.
  • 人同 - Creature resembling a monkey but not a monkey, with a specific name in Chinese and a local name in the native language.
  • 穹庐 - Yurt, a traditional portable tent used by nomadic peoples in Central Asia.
  • 噶里 - Local name for the creature, indicating its presence in the region.

Proper Nouns

  • 貘 - Mythical creature.
  • 人同 - Creature resembling a monkey.
  • 噶里 - Local name for the creature.
  • 穹庐 - Yurt.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鸭嬖 - A term used to describe a person who is favored or pampered, likely with a connotation of indulgence or excess.
  • 微有姿 - Literally means 'slightly having beauty' or 'slightly attractive,' used to describe someone who is not exceptionally beautiful but has a certain charm.
  • 性柔和 - Means 'gentle disposition' or 'soft nature,' describing a person's temperament.
  • 狎之者 - Refers to those who are intimate or familiar with someone, often in a sexual context.
  • 无所拒 - Means 'without refusal' or 'not rejecting,' indicating that the person does not refuse advances.
  • 忽一雄鸭飞啮其臀 - A vivid and somewhat surreal description of an event where a male duck suddenly flies and bites the person's buttocks.
  • 尾扑之作抽叠状 - Describes the action of the duck's tail flapping in a way that resembles a rhythmic or repetitive motion.
  • 击之不去 - Means 'cannot be driven away' or 'cannot be chased off,' indicating that the person cannot get rid of the duck.
  • 尾后拖下肉茎一缕 - Describes a piece of flesh or tissue trailing from the person's tail, likely a humorous or exaggerated description.
  • 臊水涓涓然 - Means 'smelly water dripping' or 'a trickle of foul-smelling liquid,' describing a bodily fluid.
  • 合署人大笑 - Means 'everyone in the office laughed,' indicating a shared reaction to the incident.
  • 美风姿 - Means 'beautiful appearance' or 'graceful demeanor,' describing someone's attractive physical presence.
  • 家住水沟头，密迩圣庙 - Means 'lives near the water ditch, close to the sacred temple,' providing a geographical context.
  • 多为游人憩息 - Means 'often used by tourists for resting,' indicating the bridge's function.
  • 日将夕 - Means 'the day is nearing its end' or 'it is getting late in the day,' indicating the time of day.
  • 步入学宫 - Means 'walks into the school,' indicating the person's movement.
  • 见间道侧一小门 - Means 'sees a small door on the side of a side road,' describing the location.
  • 女徘徊户下 - Means 'a woman is lingering by the door,' indicating the woman's presence.
  • 生心动 - Means 'the man's heart is stirred,' indicating an emotional reaction.
  • 试前乞火 - Means 'tries to borrow a light' or 'asks for a light,' indicating a polite request.
  • 女笑而与之 - Means 'the woman smiles and gives it to him,' indicating a positive response.
  • 亦以目相注 - Means 'also looks at him with her eyes,' indicating mutual interest.
  • 生更欲进词 - Means 'the man wants to say more,' indicating a desire to continue the conversation.
  • 而女已阖扉 - Means 'but the woman has already closed the door,' indicating the end of the interaction.
  • 遂记门径而出 - Means 'and then remembers the path and leaves,' indicating the man's departure.
  • 次日再往 - Means 'the next day, he goes again,' indicating a repeat visit.
  • 女已在门相待 - Means 'the woman is already waiting at the door,' indicating her readiness for the visit.
  • 生叩姓氏 - Means 'the man asks for her name,' indicating a formal inquiry.
  • 知为学中门斗女 - Means 'knows she is the daughter of the gatekeeper of the school,' providing information about the woman's background.
  • 且曰 - Means 'and says,' indicating a continuation of the conversation.
  • 妾舍逼隘 - Means 'my place is cramped and narrow,' indicating the woman's living conditions.
  • 不避耳目 - Means 'not avoiding eyes and ears,' indicating that the place is not private.
  • 卿家咫尺 - Means 'your home is nearby,' indicating proximity.
  • 但得静僻一室 - Means 'if only there is a quiet and secluded room,' indicating a desire for privacy.
  • 妾当夜分相就 - Means 'I will come to you in the middle of the night,' indicating a plan for a nighttime meeting.
  • 卿明夕可待我于门 - Means 'you can wait for me at the door tomorrow night,' indicating a specific time and place for the meeting.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鸭嬖 - A term that combines the idea of a favorite or pampered person with the imagery of a duck, possibly a humorous or derogatory term.
  • 圣庙 - Refers to a sacred temple, likely a Confucian temple or a place of worship.
  • 学宫 - Refers to a school or educational institution, often associated with Confucian learning.
  • 门斗 - Refers to a gatekeeper or doorkeeper, a person responsible for guarding the entrance to a place.
  • 夜分 - Means 'midnight' or 'the middle of the night,' indicating a specific time for the meeting.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨贵 - The name of the person who is nicknamed '鸭嬖'.
  • 华生 - The name of the person described as having a beautiful appearance.
  • 水沟头 - The location where the woman lives, near the water ditch.
  • 圣庙 - The sacred temple mentioned in the text.
  • 学宫 - The school or educational institution mentioned in the text.
  • 门斗 - The gatekeeper or doorkeeper of the school.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生喜急归 - Literally 'the man was delighted and hurriedly returned,' indicating a sense of urgency and excitement.
  • 诳妇以畏暑 - Literally 'deceived his wife by saying he was afraid of the heat,' suggesting a fabricated excuse.
  • 宜独寝 - Literally 'it is appropriate to sleep alone,' implying a recommendation or justification.
  • 洒扫外室 - Literally 'swept and cleaned the outer room,' indicating preparation for a guest.
  • 潜候于门 - Literally 'waited secretly at the door,' suggesting stealth and anticipation.
  • 女果夜来 - Literally 'the woman indeed came at night,' confirming an expectation.
  • 携手入室 - Literally 'held hands and entered the room,' indicating a close and intimate action.
  • 生喜过望 - Literally 'the man's joy exceeded his expectations,' suggesting a positive surprise.
  • 自是每夕必至 - Literally 'from then on, she came every night,' indicating a regular occurrence.
  • 数月后，生渐羸弱 - Literally 'after several months, the man gradually became weak,' indicating a decline in health.
  • 父母潜窥寝处 - Literally 'the parents secretly peered into his sleeping place,' suggesting covert observation.
  • 见生与女并坐嬉笑 - Literally 'saw the man and the woman sitting together and laughing,' indicating a shared activity.
  • 亟排闼入 - Literally 'hurriedly pushed open the door and entered,' suggesting urgency and forcefulness.
  • 寂然无人 - Literally 'there was no one there,' indicating a sudden disappearance.
  • 严诘生 - Literally 'severely questioned the man,' suggesting a stern interrogation.
  • 生备道始末 - Literally 'the man thoroughly explained the beginning and end,' indicating a detailed account.
  • 父母大骇 - Literally 'the parents were greatly frightened,' suggesting a strong emotional reaction.
  • 偕生赴学宫踪迹 - Literally 'accompanied the man to the school to track down,' indicating a joint effort to investigate.
  • 绝无向时门径 - Literally 'there were no traces of the previous entrance,' suggesting a complete absence of evidence.
  • 遍访门斗中 - Literally 'visited every door and corner,' indicating a thorough search.
  • 共知为妖 - Literally 'everyone knew it was a demon,' suggesting a consensus on the nature of the problem.
  • 乃广延僧道，请符 - Literally 'then widely invited monks and Taoists to bring talismans and charms,' indicating a superstitious response.
  • 一无所效 - Literally 'nothing was effective,' suggesting a failure of the measures.
  • 其父研朱砂与生曰 - Literally 'his father ground cinnabar and told the man,' indicating a specific action and instruction.
  • 俟其来时 - Literally 'wait for her to come,' suggesting anticipation.
  • 潜印女身 - Literally 'secretly imprint her body,' indicating a covert marking.
  • 便可踪迹 - Literally 'then you can track her,' suggesting a method for locating her.
  • 俟女睡 - Literally 'wait for the woman to sleep,' indicating a specific timing.
  • 以朱砂散置发上 - Literally 'spread cinnabar on her hair,' indicating a specific action.
  • 而女不知 - Literally 'but the woman did not know,' suggesting a lack of awareness.
  • 次日，父母偕人入圣庙遍寻 - Literally 'the next day, the parents accompanied people to the sacred temple and searched everywhere,' indicating a thorough search.
  • 绝无影响 - Literally 'there was no trace,' suggesting a complete absence of evidence.
  • 忽闻邻妇诟小儿曰 - Literally 'suddenly heard a neighbor woman scolding a child, saying,' indicating a sudden and unexpected event.
  • 甫换新裤 - Literally 'just changed into new pants,' suggesting a recent action.
  • 又染猩红 - Literally 'again stained with bright red,' indicating a specific color.
  • 从何处染来耶 - Literally 'where did you get this from?' indicating a question.
  • 其父闻而异之 - Literally 'his father heard it and found it strange,' suggesting a reaction to the unexpected.
  • 往视 - Literally 'went to see,' indicating an action.
  • 小儿裤上尽朱砂 - Literally 'the child's pants were entirely stained with cinnabar,' indicating a specific observation.
  • 因究儿所自 - Literally 'therefore investigated where the child got it from,' indicating an inquiry.
  • 适骑学宫前负碑龟首 - Literally 'just rode on the back of the turtle-shaped stone at the front of the school,' indicating a specific location and action.
  • 不觉染此 - Literally 'unconsciously got stained with this,' indicating an unintentional action.
  • 往视之首 - Literally 'went to see the head of the turtle-shaped stone,' indicating an action.
  • 朱砂在焉 - Literally 'cinnabar was there,' indicating a specific observation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 学宫 - Refers to a school or educational institution.
  • 圣庙 - Refers to a temple dedicated to a revered figure or deity.
  • 僧道 - Refers to monks and Taoist priests, indicating a superstitious or religious response.
  • 符 - Refers to talismans and charms, indicating a superstitious or magical approach.
  • 朱砂 - Refers to cinnabar, a red mineral used in traditional Chinese medicine and magic.
  •  - Refers to a turtle-shaped stone, often used as a marker or monument.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 予訴之母 - I complained to my mother. '訴之' means 'to complain to' or 'to tell.'
  • 母恐我泄其事 - My mother feared that I would reveal the matter. '泄其事' means 'to reveal the matter.'
  • 挞予至死 - She beat me to death. '挞' means 'to beat,' and '至死' means 'to death.'
  • 是致予死者，此妇也 - This is what led to my death, this woman. '是致' means 'this is what led to,' and '予死者' means 'my death.'
  • 我仇之久矣 - I have hated her for a long time. '仇之' means 'to hate.'
  • 今始寻着 - Now I have finally found her. '寻着' means 'to find.'
  • 报冤索命事，都是东岳掌管 - Matters of revenge and taking lives are all under the jurisdiction of the Eastern Peak. '报冤索命' means 'revenge and taking lives,' and '东岳' refers to the Eastern Peak, a place of worship in Chinese mythology.
  • 必须诉于岳帝，允救，方可以法治 - One must appeal to the Emperor of the Eastern Peak, seek his permission, and then it can be handled according to the law. '诉于岳帝' means 'to appeal to the Emperor of the Eastern Peak,' and '允救' means 'to seek permission.'
  • 否则难救 - Otherwise, it is difficult to save. '否则' means 'otherwise,' and '难救' means 'difficult to save.'
  • 默诉其事，占得上上签 - I silently appealed to the matter and drew the topmost sign. '默诉' means 'to silently appeal,' and '占得上上签' means 'to draw the topmost sign.'
  • 归告道人 - I returned and told the Taoist priest. '归告' means 'to return and tell.'
  • 其时妇胎已堕 - At that time, the woman's fetus had already fallen. '胎已堕' means 'the fetus had already fallen.'
  • 道人嫌不洁，不肯入房 - The Taoist priest disliked the impurity and would not enter the room. '嫌不洁' means 'disliked the impurity,' and '不肯入房' means 'would not enter the room.'
  • 沈合家哭求 - Shen's whole family wept and begged. '合家' means 'the whole family,' and '哭求' means 'to weep and beg.'
  • 道人乃诣榻前，书召彩云符一纸 - The Taoist priest then went to the bedside and wrote a summons to the Rainbow Cloud on a piece of paper. '诣榻前' means 'went to the bedside,' and '书召彩云符一纸' means 'wrote a summons to the Rainbow Cloud on a piece of paper.'
  • 问：“好看否？” - He asked, 'Is it beautiful?' '好看' means 'beautiful,' and '否' means 'no.'
  • 病妇答曰：“好。” - The sick woman answered, 'Yes.' '病妇' means 'sick woman,' and '答曰' means 'answered.'
  • 道人曰：“何不出现？” - The Taoist priest said, 'Why don't you appear?' '何不' means 'why don't,' and '出现' means 'appear.'
  • 应曰：“诺。” - She replied, 'Yes.' '应曰' means 'replied,' and '诺' means 'yes.'
  • 道人即捏诀向空一捉，曰：“得矣。” - The Taoist priest then made a gesture, reached out into the air, and said, 'Got it!' '捏诀' means 'to make a gesture,' '向空一捉' means 'reached out into the air,' and '得矣' means 'got it.'
  • 驰下楼去 - He rushed downstairs. '驰下楼' means 'rushed downstairs.'
  • 病人昏迷若醒，曰：“我为何遍身痛极？” - The patient was in a state of unconsciousness but seemed to be awake and said, 'Why am I in so much pain all over?' '昏迷若醒' means 'in a state of unconsciousness but seemed to be awake,' and '遍身痛极' means 'in so much pain all over.'

  • The sentence '然打者自打，说者自说。' means 'but the one who hits hits, and the one who speaks speaks.'
  • The sentence '求神惩治。' means 'ask the god to punish.'

  • 专以打捞货物为生 - '专以...为生' means 'devote themselves to making a living by...' referring to the practice of salvaging goods from sunken ships.

  • 言定价钱，一齐入水 - '言定价钱' means 'agreed on the price,' and '一齐入水' means 'went into the water together.'
  • 忽少一人 - '忽少一人' means 'suddenly one person was missing.'
  • 众疑其在水藏匿金银 - '众疑其在水藏匿金银' means 'the crowd suspected that he hid gold and silver in the water.'
  • 复入水，遍寻不得 - '复入水' means 'went back into the water,' and '遍寻不得' means 'searched everywhere but could not find.'
  • 但见一龟：赤色，大过浴盆，形扁如簸箕，无头无尾无足 - '但见一龟' means 'only saw a turtle,' '赤色' means 'red,' '大过浴盆' means 'larger than a bathtub,' '形扁如簸箕' means 'flat like a winnowing basket,' and '无头无尾无足' means 'no head, no tail, no feet.'
  • 水鬼被其咬住，拉之不开 - '水鬼被其咬住' means 'the water ghost was bitten by it,' and '拉之不开' means 'could not pull it open.'
  • 乃以大铁钩拽龟上岸 - '乃以大铁钩拽龟上岸' means 'then used a large iron hook to drag the turtle onto the shore.'
  • 通体有小穴数百，皆其口也 - '通体有小穴数百' means 'there were hundreds of small holes all over its body,' and '皆其口也' means 'all were its mouths.'
  • 人血已经吸尽，而口犹紧咬不放 - '人血已经吸尽' means 'the human blood had been sucked dry,' and '而口犹紧咬不放' means 'but the mouths still tightly held on.'
  • 刺以利刃，龟若不知 - '刺以利刃' means 'stabbed with a sharp blade,' and '龟若不知' means 'the turtle seemed unaware.'
  • 不得已，并人与龟烈火焚之，臭闻数里 - '不得已' means 'unavoidably,' '并人与龟' means 'both the person and the turtle,' '烈火焚之' means 'burned with intense fire,' and '臭闻数里' means 'the smell spread for several miles.'

  • 小姐亦附其妻身大骂 - Means 'Lady Lu also attached herself to Wang's wife and scolded her.'
  • 举家哀求，欲延高僧为设斋醮 - Means 'The whole family begged and wanted to invite a high monk to perform a ritual.'
  • 不必，汝村农无知 - Means 'It's unnecessary, you village farmers are ignorant.'
  • 既自知罪，但速将鼎、镜等物送归原所，别买棺安葬我骨，可以恕汝 - Means 'Since you know your guilt, just quickly return the tripod, mirror, and other items to their original place, and buy another coffin to bury my bones, and I will forgive you.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一目五先生 - A compound term referring to a group of five ghosts, one of whom has one eye and the others are blind. This is a unique and specific cultural reference.
  • 浙中有五奇鬼 - Five strange ghosts in Zhejiang. This phrase introduces the setting and the characters.
  • 四鬼尽瞽 - Four of the ghosts are completely blind. This emphasizes the unusual nature of the group.
  • 惟一鬼有一眼 - Only one ghost has one eye. This highlights the unique feature of the group.
  • 群鬼恃以看物 - The other ghosts rely on this one to see. This explains the dependency of the group.
  • 號“一目五先生” - They are called 'One-Eyed Five Gentlemen.' This is the name given to the group.
  • 遇瘟疫之年 - During years of plague. This sets the context for the story.
  • 五鬼聯袂而行 - The five ghosts walk together. This describes their behavior.
  • 伺人熟睡 - They wait for people to fall asleep. This explains their method of operation.
  • 以鼻嗅之 - They sniff with their noses. This describes their method of identifying victims.
  • 一鬼嗅則其人病 - If one ghost sniffs, the person falls ill. This explains the effect of one ghost's action.
  • 五鬼共嗅則其人死 - If all five ghosts sniff, the person dies. This explains the combined effect of all ghosts.
  • 四鬼伥伥然斜行踯躅 - The four blind ghosts walk aimlessly and hesitantly. This describes their movement.
  • 不敢作主 - They dare not make decisions. This emphasizes their dependence on the one-eyed ghost.
  • 惟听一目先生之号令 - They only follow the commands of the one-eyed master. This highlights the leadership role of the one-eyed ghost.
  • 有钱某宿旅店中 - There was a man named Qian staying in an inn. This introduces the main character.
  • 群客皆寐 - All the guests were asleep. This sets the scene.
  • 己独未眠 - He alone was awake. This creates a contrast.
  • 灯忽缩小 - The lamp suddenly shrank. This is a supernatural element.
  • 见五鬼排跳而至 - He saw the five ghosts jump and come. This describes the appearance of the ghosts.
  • 先生曰：“此大善人也，不可。” - The master said, 'This is a great virtuous person, we cannot do it.' This shows the master's moral judgment.
  • 又将嗅一客 - They were about to sniff another guest. This describes the next action.
  • 先生曰：“此大有福人也，不可。” - The master said, 'This is a great fortunate person, we cannot do it.' This shows the master's continued moral judgment.
  • 先生曰：“此大恶人也，更不可。” - The master said, 'This is a great wicked person, even more so, we cannot do it.' This shows the master's final moral judgment.
  • 四鬼曰：“然则先生将何餐？” - The four ghosts said, 'Then what will the master eat?' This shows the ghosts' concern.
  • 先生指二客曰：“此辈不善不恶、无福无禄，不啖何待？” - The master pointed to two guests and said, 'These people are neither good nor bad, neither fortunate nor blessed, why wait to eat them?' This shows the master's decision.
  • 四鬼即群嗅之 - The four ghosts immediately sniffed them. This describes the final action.
  • 二客鼻声渐微 - The two guests' breathing gradually became faint. This shows the effect of the ghosts' action.
  • 五鬼腹渐膨亨矣 - The five ghosts' bellies gradually became swollen. This shows the result of their meal.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 浙中 refers to the Zhejiang region, a province in eastern China known for its rich cultural heritage.
  • 瘟疫 refers to a plague or epidemic, a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 鬼 refers to ghosts, supernatural beings that are prevalent in Chinese mythology and folklore.
  • 先生 is a respectful title often used for scholars, teachers, or leaders.
  • 一目 is a term that can be interpreted as 'one-eyed,' emphasizing the unique characteristic of the ghost.
  • 五先生 is a collective term for the five ghosts, suggesting a group of respected or feared figures.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃檄福、泉二知府，会鞫于省中城隍庙 - He then issued a decree to the governors of Fu and Quan, ordering them to convene at the provincial city god temple for a joint investigation; describes the official action taken to address the incident.

My family has suffered an extraordinary injustice, and the deity shows no response. How can you enjoy human sacrifices?'; conveys the son's desperate plea and the perceived lack of divine intervention.
  • 语毕，庙之西厢突然倾倒 - After speaking, the western wing of the temple suddenly collapsed; describes the dramatic and unexpected event that occurred.

  • 于是观者大噪，两府亦悚然重鞫，始白其子冤，而置王监生于法 - The crowd then made a great commotion, and the two governors, startled, re-investigated the case, clearing the son's name and punishing Wang Jiansheng according to the law; describes the outcome of the re-investigation.

  • 南唐李后主裹足案也 - This clause is clear but could be read as 'the case of Li Houzhu of Southern Tang wrapping his feet' or 'the case involving Li Houzhu of Southern Tang and the practice of foot-binding.'
  • 后主前世本嵩山净明和尚，转身为江南国主 - This clause is clear but could be read as 'in his previous life, the Later Master was the Pure and Bright Monk of Mount Song, and in this life he became the ruler of the Jiangnan kingdom' or 'in his previous life, the Later Master was the Pure and Bright Monk of Mount Song, and he was reincarnated as the ruler of the Jiangnan kingdom.'
  • 宫中行乐，以帛裹其妃窈娘足为新月之形 - This clause is clear but could be read as 'in the palace, they engaged in amusement by wrapping his concubine Yao娘's feet in silk to form the shape of a new moon' or 'in the palace, they engaged in amusement by wrapping Yao娘's feet in silk to form the shape of a new moon.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日下午 - A straightforward time reference, but in Classical Chinese, it often implies a specific day after a significant event or action.
  • 其子忽狂叫云 - "忽" means "suddenly," and "云" is used here to introduce direct speech.
  • 甚热 - A simple expression of extreme heat, but it can be used metaphorically to describe intense emotions.
  • 我往浴于河 - "往浴于河" is a literal translation of "go to bathe in the river." In Classical Chinese, bathing in rivers was a common practice and could have ritual significance.
  • 叟不许 - "叟" refers to an elderly man, and "不许" means "refused to allow."
  • 其子竟去 - "竟去" means "went anyway," indicating defiance or disregard for the elder's wishes.
  • 狂躁愈甚 - "狂躁" describes a state of extreme agitation or madness, and "愈甚" means "even more so."
  • 指地上石云 - "指地上石" means "pointing to a stone on the ground," and "云" introduces the statement that follows.
  • 如此好水，何不令我浴 - "如此好水" means "such good water," and "何不令我浴" is a rhetorical question meaning "why not let me bathe?"
  • 光景甚怪 - "光景" can mean "appearance" or "situation," and "甚怪" means "very strange."
  • 惧不能提防 - "惧" means "fear," and "不能提防" means "unable to guard against."
  • 遍告诸邻 - "遍告" means "inform everyone," and "诸邻" means "all the neighbors."
  • 西邻唐姓者 - "西邻" means "the neighbor to the west," and "唐姓者" means "a person surnamed Tang."
  • 向信鬼神之事 - "向" means "previously," and "信鬼神之事" means "believed in matters of ghosts and spirits."
  • 里中祀东岳帝 - "里中" means "in the village," and "祀东岳帝" means "worship the Eastern Emperor (Dongyue)." Dongyue is a deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 唐主其事 - "主其事" means "in charge of the matter."
  • 或代亲友以祈禳 - "或" means "sometimes," "代亲友" means "on behalf of relatives and friends," and "以祈禳" means "to pray for blessings or ward off evil."
  • 屡屡应验 - "屡屡" means "repeatedly," and "应验" means "verified" or "proved effective."
  • 帝君圣诞日 - "帝君" means "Emperor God," and "圣诞日" means "birthday" (in the context of deities).
  • 各执事俱齐 - "各执事" means "all officials" or "all those in charge," and "俱齐" means "all present."
  • 汝具牒呈焚香炉内 - "汝" means "you," "具牒" means "prepare a document," "呈" means "present," and "焚香炉内" means "in the incense burner."
  • 我鸣钟鼓相助 - "我" means "I," "鸣钟鼓" means "sound the bells and drums," and "相助" means "to assist."
  • 令有力者抱令郎在堂下 - "令" means "order," "有力者" means "someone strong," "抱" means "hold," "令郎" means "your son," and "在堂下" means "in the hall."
  • 听候审讯发落 - "听候" means "await," "审讯" means "interrogation," and "发落" means "judgment" or "disposition."
  • 或可驱除恶鬼 - "或" means "perhaps," "可" means "can," "驱除" means "expel," and "恶鬼" means "evil spirits."

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 东岳帝 - Dongyue is a deity in Chinese mythology, specifically the god of Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains in China. Worshiping Dongyue was a common practice in ancient China.
  • 鬼神之事 - Belief in ghosts and spirits is a significant aspect of traditional Chinese culture, often involving rituals and prayers to appease or communicate with these entities.
  • 帝君圣诞日 - The birthday of a deity, particularly in the context of religious festivals, is a time of special significance and celebration.
  • 鸣钟鼓 - The use of bells and drums in religious ceremonies is a common practice in Chinese culture, often to summon attention or to accompany prayers and rituals.
  • 堂下 - The hall of a house, where important family gatherings and ceremonies take place, is a central location in traditional Chinese homes.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 居月餘，謝歸其喪 - This clause is somewhat elliptical but can be understood as 'after staying for a month, the body was returned for burial.'
  • 解缆時，同里人附一柩于船尾，謝家人不知也 - This clause introduces a sudden and unexpected event, which is crucial to the narrative.
  • 出粤界後，舟子欺其孤孀，與家人爭殴 - This clause sets up a conflict between the boatman and the family, which is resolved by the intervention of Lu's nephew and the young man.
  • 從此一路，舟人倍小心矣 - This clause indicates a change in behavior by the boatmen after the incident.
  • 舟抵家，家人为開喪設主，從此寂然 - This clause marks the end of the narrative with a return to normalcy and the completion of the funeral rites.

  • 故意杀风景以逢迎之 - 'Intentionally destroying the scenery to curry favor with him.'

  • 淫虽非礼 - 'Though lust is not proper conduct.'

  • 不比人间他罪难忏悔也 - 'It is not as difficult to repent as other sins in the mortal world.'
